description: Residual Stream Probing Layer - Train a probing MLP to measure H-level information gain by comparing z_H (before) and z_H* (after) residual streams at each ACT step

userStories.title: Implement the ProbingMLP module
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want a probing MLP class so that I can decode digit predictions from concatenated residual streams.
acceptanceCriteria: New file models/probing.py with a ProbingMLP class (nn.Module)
Architecture: Linear(d_in, hidden_mult*d_in) -> ReLU -> Linear(hidden_mult*d_in, 9) where d_in = 3 * hidden_size (default 1536)
Output is logits over 9 classes (digits 1-9), output shape [batch, seq, 9] for input [batch, seq, d_in]
hidden_mult is configurable (default 4), hidden_size is configurable (default 512)
Weight initialization uses trunc_normal_init_ following existing codebase conventions
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Extend HookContext with input_embeddings field
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need access to input_embeddings inside hook callbacks so that probing and future hooks can use them without re-computing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add input_embeddings: torch.Tensor field to the HookContext dataclass in hrm_act_v2.py
Pass input_embeddings (computed at the start of forward()) to every HookContext construction site (4 sites: 2 no-grad L/H hooks, 2 grad-step L/H hooks)
Existing hooks in hrm_inspect.py continue to work unchanged (they simply ignore the new field)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create probe_train.py with residual stream extraction hooks
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need hook callbacks that capture z_H (before H-level) and z_H* (after H-level) plus z_L and input_embedding at each ACT step, so that I can feed them to the probe.
acceptanceCriteria: New file probe_train.py with hook callback functions for residual stream extraction
L-hook callback captures z_H_before (ctx.z_H at last l_cycle, which is carry-in z_H before H-level call) and z_L (ctx.z_L at last l_cycle)
H-hook callback captures z_H_after (ctx.z_H at last h_cycle, which is z_H* after H-level call)
input_embeddings captured from ctx.input_embeddings (added in US-002)
Data collected for ACT steps >= skip_steps (default 2), skipping early steps where z_H/z_L are not yet stable
Extracted tensors are detached and optionally moved to CPU to avoid OOM
Hooks registered via existing register_hook_H / register_hook_L mechanism
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement probe training loop with CLI
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want a training script that loads a frozen HRM checkpoint, extracts residual streams via hooks, and trains the probing MLP so I can measure H-level information gain.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI interface in probe_train.py with argparse: --checkpoint, --data, --lr (default 1e-3), --weight-decay (default 1e-2), --epochs, --batch-size, --act-steps, --skip-act-steps (default 2), --mode train/eval
Loads frozen HRM checkpoint using existing load_model() from hrm_inspect.py, sets eval() and requires_grad_(False)
For each batch: runs HRM forward with hooks, forms probe inputs concat(z_H, z_L, input_emb) and concat(z_H*, z_L, input_emb) along hidden dim -> [batch, 81, 1536] (strips puzzle prefix position 0)
Passes both inputs through the SAME ProbingMLP -> logits [batch, 81, 9]
Cross-entropy loss computed on blank cells only (where inputs == 1); target labels remapped from tokens 2-10 to classes 0-8
Combined loss = loss(z_H input) + loss(z_H* input), both contribute gradients to same probe
AdamW optimizer on probe parameters only with configurable lr and weight_decay
Logs training loss and accuracy per epoch to stdout and optionally to Wandb
Runnable as: python probe_train.py --checkpoint <path> --data <path> --mode train
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add probe evaluation mode with per-step metrics
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to evaluate the trained probe and see per-ACT-step accuracy for z_H vs z_H*, so I can quantify H-level information gain.
acceptanceCriteria: Evaluation mode activated via --mode eval in probe_train.py
Reports per-ACT-step metrics separately for z_H and z_H* (for steps >= skip_steps): per-cell accuracy on blank cells, cross-entropy loss, and accuracy delta acc(z_H*) - acc(z_H)
Prints a summary table: ACT step x {z_H acc, z_H* acc, delta} starting from the configured skip step
Optionally logs metrics to Wandb with step-indexed keys
Requires --probe-checkpoint flag to load a saved probe
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add probe checkpoint saving and loading
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to save and load trained probe weights so I can reuse them without retraining.
acceptanceCriteria: Saves probe state_dict + config (hidden_size, hidden_mult, num_classes) to a .pt checkpoint file
Saves at configurable interval (--save-interval) and always at end of training
Loading reconstructs ProbingMLP architecture from saved config, then loads state_dict
CLI flag --probe-checkpoint to resume training or run eval from a saved probe
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add unit tests for probe module and data pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for the probing MLP and the data remapping logic so that correctness is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tests/test_probing.py
Test that ProbingMLP output shape is [batch, seq, 9] for input shape [batch, seq, 1536]
Test that target label remapping converts tokens 2-10 to classes 0-8 correctly
Test that blank cell mask correctly identifies cells where inputs == 1
Test that loss is computed only on masked (blank) cells and given cells do not contribute to loss
Test that probe gradients flow only to probe parameters, not HRM parameters
pytest tests/test_probing.py passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
